title: Lelya prostrata
intro:
There is no available information on a plant called "Lelya prostrata." However, I found information on the plant "Lycopodiella prostrata", also known as Lycopodiella cernua, a type of club moss. 

Lycopodiella cernua is a low-growing, creeping plant that belongs to the family Lycopodiaceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and infections.

The bioactive constituents of Lycopodiella cernua include lignans, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

Lycopodiella cernua is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
